title: Mutations before selection - the jackpot test
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Luria and Delbruck's 1943 Nobel experiment, in silico: grow 2000 identical cultures and count resistant mutants. Most have a handful, but a few 'jackpot' cultures - where a mutation happened early and founded a huge clone - have hundreds. That wild variance (far above the mean) proved mutations arise at random during growth, not in response to selection.
